data.title: France's Economy Stalled in First Quarter as Energy Shock Weighs — Update
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

The French economy had an unexpectedly weak start to the year, and is likely to struggle ahead as a jump in energy prices weakens consumer spending and delays a hoped for recovery in industry.

The conflict in the Middle East is the latest blow to Europe's hopes for a sustained revival in its economic fortunes, after Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022 and President Trump's tariff blitz last year.

Gross domestic product in France--the eurozone's second-largest member--was flat in the first quarter of the year, compared with 0.2% growth in the final quarter of 2025, the country's statistics agency Insee said Thursday. A consensus of economists polled by The Wall Street Journal also expected 0.2% growth.

Household consumption fell back 0.1%, while fixed capital investment declined 0.4% and foreign trade damped GDP growth after exports fell sharply, Insee said.

As a net importer of energy, Europe is more exposed to the surging energy prices driven by conflict in the Middle East than the U.S. The European Union has spent an extra 27 billion euros on energy imports due to higher prices since the first strikes on Iran at the end of February, European Commission president Ursula von der Leyen said Wednesday.

The European Central Bank, which announces its interest-rate decision later Thursday, last month cut its forecast for eurozone growth this year to 0.9% from the 1.2% it predicted in December, and trimmed its forecast for 2027.

But the slowdown is likely to be more severe if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed to shipping for much of what remains of the year. Brent crude oil this week climbed above $120 a barrel, reflecting skepticism about the prospects for an early resumption of oil and natural gas flows through the choke point.

ECB President Christine Lagarde said earlier this month that the eurozone is currently between the bank's baseline and an adverse scenario that predicts a weaker 0.6% GDP growth this year. An even more severe scenario envisaged 0.4% growth.

"We are definitely moving towards the adverse scenario," said Carsten Brzeski, global head of macro for ING Research.

Surveys show Europeans are already feeling more fearful about the future. Consumer confidence in the eurozone sank to its lowest since December 2022, a time when inflation in the currency area was coming off double-digit highs.

Business confidence has also worsened. Surveys of purchasing managers in the eurozone showed activity at its weakest in 17 months in April, with output contracting with input and selling prices rising at their sharpest rates in more than three years.

A fresh headwind might come in the form of higher interest rates. While the ECB has yet to respond to the pickup in inflation, investors expect it to act soon if there isn't any sign of a cooling in energy prices.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: Air France-KLM Q1 revenue rises; says high fuel prices to hit Q2, cuts FY capacity view
data.description:
Overview

  • Airline group's Q1 revenue rose 4.4% yr/yr to €7.5 bln

  • Operating loss narrowed to €27 mln, supported by higher ticket prices and premiumization

  • Company says fuel price increases from Middle East conflict not yet visible in Q1 results

Outlook

  • Air France-KLM now sees 2026 capacity up 2% to 4% vs 2025, down from 3% to 5%

  • Company expects 2026 unit cost up 0% to 2%, including 0.5% from premiumization

  • Company forecasts 2026 fuel bill at USD 9.3 bln, up USD 2.4 bln from 2025

Result Drivers

  • PASSENGER NETWORK & PREMIUMIZATION - Group revenue growth and improved operating result were driven by strong passenger network performance, higher ticket prices and premiumization, especially in front cabins

  • CAPACITY REALLOCATION & INDUSTRY DISRUPTION - Reduced industry capacity in March due to the Middle East conflict and reallocation of group capacity to Asia and East Africa supported yields and operating result

  • COST CONTROL & PRODUCTIVITY - Unit cost increase limited to 0.5% as higher customer compensation and premiumization were offset by disciplined cost management and productivity gains

Company press release:

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

Q1 Revenue

EUR 7.48 bln

Q1 Net Income

-EUR 252 mln

Q1 Operating Margin

-0.40%

Q1 Operating result

-EUR 27 mln

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy", 13 "hold" and 4 "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the airlines peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Air France KLM SA is €10.20, about 16.9% above its April 29 closing price of €8.73

  • The stock recently traded at 2 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 3 three months ago

For questions concerning the data in this report, contact Estimates.Support@lseg.com. For any other questions or feedback, contact reuters.support@thomsonreuters.com.

data.title: Premier Inn owner Whitbread extends restaurant overhaul to all sites, to cut 3,800 jobs
data.description: Britain's Whitbread on Thursday said it will replace all 197 remaining branded restaurants with food and beverage offerings, warning it could cut around 3,800 roles in the UK and Ireland.

data.title: AKTIA: Operating profit fell 35% as life insurance income dropped, but asset management and credit quality improved
data.description:
Q1 2026 saw a 35% drop in operating profit due to market-driven declines in life insurance and lower net interest income, while asset management grew and credit losses fell sharply. Outlook for 2026 remains stable, with lower credit losses expected.

Original document: Aktia Bank Plc Class A [AKTIA] Slides Release — Apr. 30 2026
